kpi_metrics.intro: Each KPI value is read from error_dashboard_data.json or processing_summary.txt. The list below describes which file and field supplies each metric.

items.label: Rows Processed
items.text: comes from error_dashboard_data.json, reading health_kpi.total_rows first and falling back to metadata.total_rows.

items.label: Match Rate
items.text: is calculated from error_dashboard_data.json by dividing summary.unique_errors by health_kpi.total_rows, then subtracting the result from 100%.

items.label: Columns Created
items.text: is parsed from processing_summary.txt by extracting the column number from the Processed Data Shape line.

items.label: Data Health
items.text: is read from error_dashboard_data.json field health_kpi.score, displayed as a percentage with a color gradient bar underneath.

pipeline_stages.intro: Stage status and progress bars are driven by the phase_breakdown array inside error_dashboard_data.json. Each pipeline stage maps to a phase code and the corresponding phase entry determines its appearance.
pipeline_stages.details: If a phase entry has its Critical count greater than zero, the stage is marked FAIL with a red progress bar. If it has a non-zero Total count but no critical issues, the stage shows a warning count with an amber bar. A phase with zero issues shows PASS with a green bar.
pipeline_stages.export_note: The Export stage has no phase code assigned, so its status is derived from the overall run: it passes only when summary.clean_run is true. Stages that do not match any phase entry fall back to no data with a grey bar.

source_file.intro: The Source File section in the left sidebar displays metadata about the input Excel file used for the current pipeline run.

fields.label: File Name
fields.text: read from debug_log.json global_parameters.upload_file_name or the filename extracted from the bootstrap path.

fields.label: Sheet Name
fields.text: taken from debug_log.json global_parameters.upload_sheet_name — identifies which worksheet was processed.

fields.label: Path
fields.text: the full path to the input file, resolved at pipeline startup from CLI args, UI overrides, or schema defaults.

debug_log_data_table.intro: Clicking an output file in the sidebar opens a detail data table in the main content area. The table supports tabbed views for JSON files and inline preview for text files.
debug_log_data_table.details: For JSON files, the first data array is shown as a sortable table with up to 50 rows. Additional tabs give access to other arrays in the file. The Raw JSON tab shows the full unparsed content. For text files, the first 10KB are displayed inline with a monospace font.

fields.label: CSV / Excel
fields.text: open in a new browser tab (no inline table preview).

fields.label: Summary
fields.text: displays the full processing_summary.txt content inline.

fields.label: Debug Log
fields.text: the messages array is shown as a table, with the errors and trace_table arrays available in additional tabs.

fields.label: Error Data
fields.text: the phase_breakdown and error_types arrays are shown as tables, plus a Raw JSON tab.

execution.intro: The Execution section in the left sidebar displays run-level metadata collected at pipeline completion.

fields.label: Last Run
fields.text: timestamp from debug_log.json timestamp field, formatted as a human-readable date/time.

fields.label: Schema
fields.text: the schema file version read from debug_log.json bootstrap parameters.

fields.label: Status
fields.text: derived from error_dashboard_data.json — READY if ready is true, otherwise FAILED.

fields.label: Health
fields.text: the data health score and grade from error_dashboard_data.json health_kpi object.

output_files.text: Each output file link points to a file inside dcc/output/ and opens in a new browser tab.

submittal_data_source.intro: The Submittal Tracker loads data from the processed pipeline CSV output.

fields.label: Load CSV
fields.text: click the Load CSV button to pick a local CSV file via FileReader. The Refresh button fetches the default file from ../output/processed_dcc_universal.csv. Loaded row count and source filename appear in the toolbar and status bar.

fields.label: Data Info
fields.text: the left sidebar Data Info section shows the total row count, column count, and source filename after loading.

submittal_analytics.intro: Four KPI tiles summarize the loaded submission data at a glance. All values are computed per unique Document_ID from the active (filtered) data set. Click any KPI card to view its detail table below the charts.

fields.label: Total Documents
fields.text: distinct count of Document_ID values. Not affected by duplicate submission rows. Click to view document list.

fields.label: Open Submissions
fields.text: unique documents where Submission_Closed is not YES — submissions still awaiting final closure. A doc is considered closed if any of its rows has Submission_Closed=YES. Click to view open documents.

fields.label: Overdue
fields.text: unique documents that have at least one row where Resubmission_Overdue_Status equals Overdue — resubmissions past their planned return date. Click to view overdue details.

fields.label: Approval Rate
fields.text: percentage of unique documents with a terminal approval code (APP, A, APR, APPROVED) among all documents that have an approval code on record. Click to view approval breakdown.

submittal_timeline.intro: The Submission Timeline chart shows unique document volume over time.
submittal_timeline.details: Unique Document_ID values are grouped by the year-month portion of Submission_Date. A document with multiple submissions in the same month is counted once. The line chart uses the current theme accent color and fills the area underneath. Months with no submissions are omitted.

submittal_status_chart.intro: The Approval Status doughnut chart counts unique Document_ID values grouped by their latest approval code. Each document is counted once. Each slice label shows the category name with the document count in parentheses.

fields.label: Approved
fields.text: codes APP, APR, APPROVED, A — submission approved.

fields.label: AWC
fields.text: code AWC (Approved with Comments) — approved subject to comments.

fields.label: Rejected
fields.text: codes REJ, R, REJECTED, NAP (Not Approved).

fields.label: Pending
fields.text: codes PEN, PENDING — awaiting a decision.

fields.label: Void
fields.text: code VOID — submission voided or cancelled.

fields.label: Info
fields.text: code INF — for information only, no approval required.

fields.label: Other
fields.text: any unrecognised approval code not falling into the above categories.

submittal_discipline_chart.intro: The Documents by Discipline bar chart shows document counts per engineering discipline.
submittal_discipline_chart.details: Each bar represents a distinct Discipline value from the loaded data. Bars are sorted from highest to lowest count. The chart uses the current theme accent color.

submittal_trend_chart.intro: The Approval Rate Trend chart tracks approval percentage over time.
submittal_trend_chart.details: For each month, the approval rate is calculated as the number of approved rows divided by total rows with an approval code. The trend line uses the success (green) color. Values range from 0% to 100%.

submittal_filters.intro: Six filter dropdowns in the left sidebar narrow the visible data set. Filtering is applied immediately and updates all charts, KPIs, and the overdue table.

fields.label: Project
fields.text: filter by Project_Code. Populated from distinct values in the loaded data.

fields.label: Facility
fields.text: filter by Facility_Code. Populated from distinct values in the loaded data.

fields.label: Discipline
fields.text: filter by Discipline. Populated from distinct values in the loaded data.

fields.label: Department
fields.text: filter by Department. Populated from distinct values in the loaded data.

fields.label: Submitted By
fields.text: filter by Submitted_By. Populated from distinct values in the loaded data.

fields.label: Review Status
fields.text: filter by Review_Status. Populated from distinct values in the loaded data.

submittal_overdue_table.intro: The Overdue Resubmissions table lists all rows where Resubmission_Overdue_Status equals Overdue.
submittal_overdue_table.details: Rows are sorted by days overdue (highest first). Each row shows the Document_ID, Discipline, Resubmission_Plan_Date, and computed days overdue. Click column headers to toggle sort order (placeholder — reload re-sorts). An empty state message is shown when no overdue items exist.

about.title: DCC Pipeline Dashboard
about.description: Data loaded from pipeline output files.
